texts.full_text: n this undated photo made available by Britain's Channel 4 television of Oxford University genetics professor Bryan Sykes posing with a prepared DNA sample taken from hair from a Himalayan animal. DNA testing is taking a bite out of the Bigfoot legend. After scientists analyzed more than 30 hair samples reportedly left behind by Bigfoot and other related beasts like Yeti and almasty, they found all of them came from more mundane animals like bears, wolves, cows and raccoons. In 2012, researchers at Oxford University genetics professor Bryan Sykes and colleagues analyzed DNA from 19 hair samples attributed to Bigfoot, and found that they all matched those of bears. The study, published in the journal Northwestern Natural History, used genetic testing to identify the source of the hair, and the results showed that all the samples were from bears, specifically American black bears and Asiatic black bears. The findings suggested that the Bigfoot legend may be based on misidentified bear tracks and droppings, rather than a separate, unknown species of large, human-like creature. The study's lead author, Bryan Sykes, a professor of human genetics at Oxford University, noted that the DNA evidence provided a definitive answer to the Bigfoot mystery, concluding that the creature does not exist as a separate species. "The evidence is now overwhelming that there's no such thing as Bigfoot," Sykes said in a statement. "The results from DNA testing on the supposed Bigfoot hair samples provide a biological explanation for the sightings, and it's not something that's human, it's something that's bear." The findings were met with mixed reactions from Bigfoot enthusiasts and skeptics alike. While some accepted the scientific evidence, others dismissed the results, pointing out that the hair samples used in the study may not have been collected from Bigfoot, but rather from bears

texts.full_text: rs I've been sharing my photographic journey with this community and on Facebook for over a year now. The support and kind words I've received have been incredible, with many suggesting I create a photo book. Initially, I envisioned a straightforward process of selecting images, refining them, and assembling a collection. However, as I delve deeper into this project, self-doubt creeps in. Am I qualified to author a photo book? Is my work worthy of being published and purchased? Printing the photos became a critical moment of reflection. Among the many I've taken, only a select few truly resonate with me—a mere 20 images over 18 months that I genuinely love. This realization has led me to question my artistic judgment and the entire endeavor. Currently, I find myself in the midst of a creative battle, grappling with self-belief and practical considerations. While I have the photos and an understanding of potential costs, self-publishing seems increasingly complex. The process is proving to be a profound learning curve, both creatively and logistically

texts.full_text: 55, July 21, 2009 by Wsk (Talk | contribs) (diff) ← Older revision | Latest revision (diff) | Newer revision → (diff) Jump to: navigation, search His All-Holiness Joachim II was the Patriarch of Constantinople during two periods during the latter part of the nineteenth century: from 1860 to 1863 and from 1873 to 1878. Life Joachim was born in Trabzon on January 25, 1804. He was ordained a deacon at an early age, and he served as an archdeacon for Metropolitan of Trebizond Eugenius (later Patriarch Eugenios II). In 1839 Joachim was consecrated to the episcopate with the title "Metropolitan of Syrmio" by Patriarch Gregory VI of Constantinople; in 1842 he became metropolitan of Demetrias. He served as an exarch for the Holy Mountain from 1850 to 1860, and then again from 1873 to 1891. Patriarchate (first term) On May 10, 1860, Joachim was elected Patriarch of Constantinople. The Russian government opposed his election because he had been ordained by Gregory VI, who had been excommunicated in Russia and condemned as a schismatic for not recognizing the Russian Orthodox Church's independence from the Ecumenical Patriarchate. However, the Russian Foreign Minister eventually accepted Joachim II's election after receiving assurances that he would reconsider the matter of ecclesiastical autonomy. Joachim II was deposed on April 18, 1863 and replaced by Sophronius III. He was subsequently appointed metropolitan of Adrianople (Edirne). Due to number of problems related with oral, parenteral, rectal and other routes of drug administration, the interest of pharmaceutical scientists has increased towards exploring the possibilities of intranasal delivery of various drugs. Nasal drug delivery system is commonly known for the treatment of local ailments like cold, cough, rhinitis, etc. Efforts have been made to deliver various drugs, especially peptides and proteins, through nasal route for systemic use; utilizing the principles and concepts of various nanoparticulate drug delivery systems using various polymers and absorption promoters. The incorporation of drugs into nanoparticles might be a promising approach, since colloidal formulations have been shown to protect them from the degrading milieu of enzymes present in nasal mucosa, enhance their stability and bioavailability, provide controlled release patterns, reduce dosing frequency, minimize side effects, improve patient compliance. texts.full_text: s available from the stool samples from Mexico to test the 12 MXPyV-positive samples for co-infections by pan-viral microarray (ViroChip) and specific PCR analysis for diarrheal viruses. ViroChip analysis was performed as previously described [@pone.0049449-Greninger1], [@pone.0049449-Chen1]. Briefly, RNA was reverse-transcribed to cDNA using random primers (5′-GTTCCCACTGGAGGATa(N~9~)-3′) and second-strand synthesis was performed using Sequenase. Samples were labeled with Cy3 fluorescent dye, normalized to 10 pmol of incorporated dye, and hybridized overnight to the ViroChip microarray for 16 hr at 65°C. The current 8×60 k version 5.0 (v5.0) ViroChip microarrays used in this study (GEO accession number GPL15905) are manufactured commercially on an Agilent platform (Agilent Technologies), and contain 19,058 70mer oligonucleotide probes representing all viral species in GenBank. Microarrays were scanned at 2 µm resolution on an Agilent DNA Microarray Scanner. Microarray hybridization patterns were interpreted using cluster and single oligonucleotide Z-score analysis as previously described [@pone.0049449-Greninger1], [@pone.0049449-Chen1], [@pone.0049449-Chiu1], [@pone.0049449-Eisen1]. Samples were declared positive for a diarrheal virus by microarray if positive by both cluster and Z-score analysis. Diarrheal viral PCR analysis of MXPyV-positive samples from Mexico {#s2f} ------------------------------------------------------------------ PCR for 5 diarrheal viruses (calicivirus, astrovirus, adenovirus, rotavirus, and enterovirus) was performed using randomly amplified cDNA as a template. Primer pairs are listed in [Table S1](#pone.0049449.s002){ref-type="supplementary-material"}. All PCR assays were run in a total of 20 µL with 1× PCR buffer, 2 mM MgCl~2~, 0.3 mM dNTP, 10 pmol of eacH primer, and 1 unit of Taq DNA Polymerase (Invitrogen). Calicivirus, rotavirus, and enterovirus PCRs were run at 94°C×2 min; 35 cycles of 94°C for 30 s, 50°C for 30 s, 72°C for 1 min; and extension at 72°C for 5 min. Adenovirus and astrovirus PCRs were run at 94°C×2 min; 35 cycles of 94°C

Nevertheless, it remains to be elucated whether these proteins act independently or in synergy with gluten and/or its metabolites causing symptoms related to NCGS {#Sec2}. In this context, several studies have tried to identify the specific components responsible for triggering adverse reactions among wheat consumers \[[@CR6]--[@cr10]\]; however due diligence must be exercised before establishing definitive causations since co-factors such as individual genetic predisposition or environmental factors may modulate their effects. The clinical presentation of NCGS is highly heterogeneous and can encompass a wide range of gastrointestinal (GI) symptoms, extragastro intestines manifesting both physical discomforts emotional distress among affected individuals \[[@CR1], [2\[3]]-{[4]}#Sec3}. The most commonly reported GI complaints include abdominal pain/bloating and diarrhea or constipation while extraintestinal signs encompass but are not limited to dermatitis herpetiformis (DH) which is a blistering skin condition closely related with gluten intake in susceptible patients [17], neuropsychiatric disorders like depression anxiety fibromyalgia allege respiratory issues such asthma-like symptoms and arthralgia rheumatoid arthritis multiple sclerosis type 2 diabetes mellitus among others[8–9. During his reign, Bil'arab bin Sultan expanded the borders of Oman to include parts of the Hajar Mountains, which were then under the control of the Ottoman Empire. One of his notable achievements was the establishment of a stable administration in Oman, focusing on trade and diplomacy. His government encouraged foreign merchants to visit and trade with Oman, thereby enhancing the country's economic prosperity. Bil'arab bin Sultan also played an important role in promoting education and religious practices within Oman. He built several mosques and schools, fostering a strong sense of community among his people. In 1692, Bil'arab bin Sultan passed away after a reign of thirteen years. His legacy as a wise and effective ruler continues to be remembered in Omani history. His contributions to the nation's growth and development have left an indelible mark on the cultural and economic landscape of Oman. Bil'arab bin Sultan was succeeded by his son, Qasim bin Bil'arab, who continued his father's legacy of peace and prosperity in Oman. Under Qasim's leadership, Oman further expanded its tradE networks and strengthened its diplomatic relations with neighboring regions. The Yaruba dynasty continued to rule over Oman for many years, maintaining a stronghold on the country's political and cultural heritage. texts.full_text: ied. He was an angry man because he had no sons and was stuck with a single daughter. But, in his own way, I think he loved me. He used to scold my mother often. "Ramona, you good for nothing woman! Only a woman like you can't bear boys!" he'd say and she'd stay silent while looking at her toes. No wonder she died years before he did. Who wants to be reminded that they're worthless by their own husband? It was my mother who inspired me to remain a spinster. What if I had inherited her cold womb? Boys never liked me, anyway. They used to singsong lyrics like "Don't want you! Go away!" when the wind blew in our direction on Coal Island or as they walked past my house with their fathers and sisters. My father was always trying so hard for something that he could not understand: how I had become his legacy, yet still found myself at a loss. He wanted me to take over from him after school but none of it made sense in the way we were living our lives here on Coal Island; where there's nothing and nowhere else. I chose differently

texts.full_text: ion was branded as WWSB and operated with a Top 40 format. Over time, it transitioned to a classic rock format, which has been its mainstay since then. In 1984, the station was acquired by the Canales family from the former owner, and they began operating it under the new call sign WSVU (for the Canales family's surname). Under their ownership, WSVU became known as "The Surf," emphasizing its commitment to classic rock music. Throughout its history, WSVU has been recognized for its role in shaping the local music scene and promoting rock legends. The station's playlist features timeless hits from the 1960s through the 1990s, with a focus on classic rock, including iconic artists like Led Zeppelin, The Rolling Stones, and Aerosmith. Community involvement is another pillar of WSVU's identity. The station actively supports local charities, businesses, and events, often partnering with them for promotional activities or special broadcasts. Additionally, it hosts various live performances and events that celebrate the rich tapestry of classic rock music. WSVU continues to evolve as a radio station while staying true to its roots in classic rock. It maintains an online presence, allowing listeners from around the world to enjoy the station's programming. With its commitment to high-quality music and community engagement, WSVU remains a staple in West Palm Beach and beyond, offering listeners a nostalgic journey through the golden age of rock 'n' roll. texts.full_text: ic Anæmia. In the description of the condition of benign aplastic anæmia in pigs, attention is directed, among other things, to changes in the bone-marrow which seem of fundamental importance in understanding normal erythrogenesis.It is possible that an indirect van den Bergh reaction can be converted into an immediate direct by controlling the H-ion concentration suitably, either with buffer solutions or with less dissociable acids, such as acetic, used in preparing the van den Bergh reagent. The question of the interpretation of direct anindirect van den Bergh reactions has been a subject of debate among hematologists for some time now. While it is generally agreed that the presence of haptoglobin-bound hemoglobin results in a positive indirect reaction, the causes behind a positive direct reaction are less clear. Some researchers have suggested that the direct reaction may be due to the presence of free hemoglobin or other heme compounds in the serum, while others have proposed that it could indicate the presence of certain autoantibodies. In the context of aplastic anemia, where bone marrow failure leads to decreased production of red blood cells and often increased destruction (hemolysis), both free hemoglobin and haptoglobin-bound hemoglobin may be present in serum samples. Therefore, interpreting the results of van den Bergh reactions in patients with aplastic anemia can be challenging. It is crucial to consider other clinical findings and laboratory tests when evaluating these results to avoid misinterpretation or missed diagnoses. Further research into the mechanisms behind direct and indirect van den Bergh reactions, particularly in the context of hemolytic conditions like aplast

The United States is one of the top recycling countries in the world. Now, it may be hard to believe when you look at the dump sites because there’s so much trash! Clearly, it means that the country is not doing enough recycling. If people are better educated about the benefits of recycling and more people play their roles in the recycling process, close to 85 percent of the total waste in the US can be recycled. Everybody has the choice to recycle or not to recycle, but why wouldn’t you? Recycling saves energy, water and reduces pollution; it helps save money and is a great way to keep your environment clean! The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) says that cardboard boxes are one of the most recyclable items in the country, making up 30 percent of all municipal solid waste. It also notes that recycling corrugated cardboard can reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 24 percent and save water by 50 percent! texts.full_text: 4, 2012 · 8:00 am ALR Student’s Corner: GPO’s Federal Digital System (FDsys) Historically, GPOAccess, which was the Government Printing Office’s online resource, was the federal government’s resource for government documents and records, such as the United States Code and the Federal Register. Anyone who used this antiquated site, however, knew that it was less than user friendly and updated infrequently. Fortunately, GPOAccess is shutting down March 16 and will be replaced by a new site that is already up and running: GPO’s Federal Digital System, or FDsys. Unlike GPOAccess, FDsys is extremely user friendly and constantly updated, almost daily with new documents and records being added to its vast database. The FDsys website provides free online access to official federal government publications, including the Congressional Record, congressional hearings, and executive branch documents. Additionally, FDsys offers a range of features that make it easier for users to search, browse, and download government documents, such as advanced search capabilities, customizable alerts, and downloadable files in multiple formats. Overall, the transition from GPOAccess to FDsys is a significant improvement, providing users with a more efficient and effective way to access federa

texts.full_text: becomes substantially permanently anchored in the base material. Both function and longevity of such anchor stud rely, in large part, on the properties of the sleeve portion. More specifically the material forming the sleeve must be durable enough to provide suitable anchoring capability and reasonable life particularly in concrete structures that, during their service life, are subject to cracking. Heretofore, the only material deemed acceptable by the art for concrete structures subject to cracking has been stainless steel. While this material is quite appropriate for the task its cost factor is difficult to absorb. Since economic considerations are important in nearly all industries, the art is always receptive to alternative configurations. In addition to cost considerations, components made of dissimilar metals will, over time when exposed to the elements, experience degradation caused by galvanic corrosion. The galvanic corrosion typically occurs at contact points or interface zones between the components. In addition, stress corrosion cracking will occur as a result of a combination of mechanical loading and chemical attack. More specifically, stress corrosion cracking results from a particular material condition, particular (specific) attacking media, and tensile stresses. When used in concrete, which typically includes calcium chloride, sulfite, aluminum chloride, and other chemicals, mechanical fasteners made from stainless steel are prone to stress corrosion cracking. Stress corrosion cracking is exacerbated on highly cold worked stainless steel. Such cracking is hard to detect on exposed parts and is all but impossible to detect when used in a hole drille

Specialist Andrea Jesse, 24, is being deployed while her husband, Edward, who is assigned to a different element of the same battalion, is not. "It's almost harder on him than it is on me," she said. "I think he feels like he should be the one going instead of me." Jesse said her and her husband's biggest concern is that she could return from active duty and then he could be called. "We both had to set up power of attorney and our wills," she said. On top of that, Jesse is scheduled to graduate from the University of Florida on Dec. 20 with a double degree in public relations and psychology, but she will miss that ceremony, too. "After I sent out my invitations I had to call everybody and say, 'You can still go, but I won't be there,'" she said.
